summary: Explained that Codex hook trust approval should not be automated because it is the security boundary for command-running hooks. Verified local DinoBrain docs/code state: installer can register user-level hooks, enable hook runtime, run an installed-hook handshake, and provide diagnostics, but cannot bypass Codex's hook trust prompt.
decisions: Do not forge or bypass Codex hook trust approval; treat it as a required user security decision.
Keep automating safe setup surfaces: hook registration, features.hooks=true, installed-hook handshake, duplicate prevention, and diagnostics.
A future improvement can guide the user to /hooks or add a post-install trust checklist, but not silently approve trust.
next_steps: Consider adding a post-install trust guide/launcher if the user wants less manual friction.
search_queries: Codex hook trust approval user-level hook automatic trust DinoBrain installer hook diagnose verify:os
